https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse.text:
URL Source: https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/

Markdown Content:
Meta unveiled Muse Spark on April 8, 2026, its first AI model from the newly formed Meta Superintelligence Labs. The natively multimodal model handles text, images, audio, and tools, competing with leading systems from OpenAI and Google on benchmarks like GPQA and HealthBench.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Model Capabilities and Reasoning Modes

Muse Spark features three reasoning modes: Instant for quick responses, Thinking for chain-of-thought on harder problems, and Contemplating, which deploys multiple agents in parallel to solve complex tasks faster than sequential methods. It supports visual chain-of-thought, allowing step-by-step reasoning on images, and multi-agent orchestration for tasks like trip planning with subagents handling itineraries and activities simultaneously.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[2](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") The model was trained with reinforcement learning that penalizes excessive thinking time, enabling efficient performance on math and science problems.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Benchmark Performance and Health Focus

On HealthBench Hard, Muse Spark achieved 42.8, surpassing GPT-5.4 at 40.1 and Gemini 3.1 Pro at 20.6, with training data curated by over 1,000 physicians for health reasoning like nutrition and drug information.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[4](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source") It excels in visual STEM tasks, generating interactive displays and minigames, and performs strongly in multimodal reasoning under independent evaluation.[1](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")[3](https://mlq.ai/news/meta-introduces-muse-spark-as-new-ai-reasoning-model-from-superintelligence-labs/# "Click to view source")

## Deployment and Infrastructure Overhaul

https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/gro.title: Grok 5 Release Date: Latest News & What We Know (April 2026)
https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/gro.text:
URL Source: https://www.nxcode.io/resources/news/grok-5-release-date-latest-news-2026

Published Time: 2026-03-24T00:00:00.000Z

Markdown Content:
## The Latest on Grok 5 (March 2026 Update)

Grok 5 has not been released as of March 2026. [Elon Musk originally confirmed](https://phemex.com/news/article/elon-musk-confirms-grok-5-launch-for-q1-2026-36075) a Q1 2026 launch, but that window has passed. xAI's official X account now points to **Q2 2026** as the most likely release window. The model carries a reported [6 trillion parameters](https://news.aibase.com/news/22865) using a Mixture-of-Experts architecture and is training on xAI's [1-gigawatt Colossus 2 supercluster](https://www.teslarati.com/elon-musk-xai-brings-1gw-colossus-2-ai-training-cluster-online/) in Memphis, Tennessee. For context, its predecessor [Grok 4 scored 88% on GPQA Diamond](https://x.ai/news/grok-4) and 25.4% on Humanity's Last Exam.

Here is what has happened in the last 30 days:

*   **March 3, 2026** -- xAI shipped Grok 4.20 Beta 2 with five targeted fixes: better instruction following, fewer hallucinations, enhanced LaTeX support, more accurate image search, and improved multi-image rendering. This is the current publicly available model, not Grok 5.
*   **February 25, 2026** -- Grok's official X account updated its projection, pointing to **Q2 2026** as the most likely window for Grok 5's full release.
*   **February 17-18, 2026** -- Grok 4.20 Beta launched with a 4-agent system, followed immediately by Grok 4.20 Heavy with 16 specialized agents. These multi-agent architectures are widely viewed as stepping stones toward Grok 5.
*   **January 2026** -- xAI confirmed Colossus 2, its 1-gigawatt supercluster in Memphis, Tennessee, is fully operational and actively training Grok 5.

The bottom line: Grok 5 missed its Q1 deadline. The new consensus points to Q2 2026.

* * *

## Expected Timeline: When Is Grok 5 Coming Out?

Based on all available signals, here is the most likely timeline:

| Milestone | Expected Window |
| --- | --- |
| Training completion | April 2026 |
| Internal testing / red-teaming | April-May 2026 |
| Public beta (X Premium+) | May-June 2026 |
| Full API access | Q3 2026 |

**Why Q2?** Three key factors point to this window:

1.   **Colossus 2 upgrade to 1.5GW in April**: Musk confirmed that the supercluster is expanding from 1GW to 1.5GW by April 2026. This upgrade aligns with the completion of Grok 5's primary training run -- the additional capacity would support fine-tuning and inference at scale.

2.   **Polymarket odds**: The prediction market gives a 33% probability that Grok 5 ships by June 30, 2026, and only 1% by March 31. The smart money says Q2.

# Google Just Put NotebookLM Inside Gemini: Here’s What You Can Do Now

3:00 pm April 11, 2026 By [Julian Horsey](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/author/jhorsey/)

![Image 1: B2B sales prospecting setup using NotebookLM sources to draft tailored outreach messages inside Gemini chat.](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/notebooklm-gemini-unified-interface.webp)

Google’s integration of **NotebookLM** with the **Gemini AI platform** introduces a structured way to handle information and streamline tasks. As explained by Universe of AI, one standout feature is **persistent memory**, which allows the system to remember details from previous interactions. This is particularly useful for long-term projects, as it reduces repetitive data entry and helps maintain continuity in workflows.

Explore how this update enhances **workflow organization** with features such as task categorization through folders and the ability to consolidate conversations into notebooks. Learn how customizable AI instructions can assist with tasks like summarizing complex research or creating detailed reports. Gain insight into **AI Studio**, which offers tools for organizing knowledge, including mind mapping, flashcard creation and slide deck development.

## Key Features of the NotebookLM & Gemini Integration

https://www.devdiscourse.com/article/tec.title: Farms go autonomous: Agentic AI set to replace traditional precision agriculture
https://www.devdiscourse.com/article/tec.text:
URL Source: https://www.devdiscourse.com/article/technology/3867667-farms-go-autonomous-agentic-ai-set-to-replace-traditional-precision-agriculture?amp

Published Time: 2026-04-13T07:53:09+05:30

Markdown Content:
Global agriculture is facing mounting pressure to feed a population projected to exceed 9.5 billion by 2050 amid climate volatility, resource scarcity, and labor shortages. Traditional farming methods, built on uniform input use and manual monitoring, are increasingly inadequate, pushing the sector toward digitally coordinated, data-driven systems commonly described as Agriculture 4.0.

A new study published in _AgriEngineering_ outlines how a new generation of agentic AI systems could redefine precision agriculture, enabling farms to operate through coordinated, goal-driven digital agents rather than static, human-dependent workflows. The study, titled [_"Agentic AI-Based IoT Precision Agriculture Framework—Our Vision and Challenges,"_](https://doi.org/10.3390/agriengineering8040147) proposes a novel architecture that integrates artificial intelligence, Internet of Things (IoT) sensors, and autonomous systems into a unified, closed-loop agricultural model capable of continuous perception, decision-making, and action execution.

Existing systems largely function as decision-support tools, generating recommendations that still rely on human interpretation and execution. The proposed agentic AI framework moves beyond this limitation by enabling systems that can autonomously sense, decide, and act in real time, significantly increasing responsiveness in dynamic agricultural environments.

### From decision support to autonomous farming systems

The study identifies a critical limitation in current AI-driven precision agriculture systems: their reliance on centralized, episodic, and recommendation-based workflows. These systems typically collect data from sensors, satellites, and machinery, process it through machine learning models, and produce insights that require human intervention to translate into action.

While effective in improving yield, water efficiency, and input optimization, these approaches lack the responsiveness required to handle real-time changes in environmental conditions such as sudden pest outbreaks, weather fluctuations, or crop stress.

The proposed agentic AI model introduces a fundamentally different paradigm. Instead of treating AI as a passive analytical tool, it defines agricultural systems as networks of autonomous agents capable of perception, reasoning, decision-making, and execution. These agents operate continuously, forming a closed-loop system that integrates sensing, planning, and action without constant human oversight.

https://www.philschmid.de/cloud-attentio.title: Cloud Attention - Issue 19: Apple Joins the AI Race, Mistral Releases 12B LLM, and Synthetic Data Surpasses Teachers
https://www.philschmid.de/cloud-attentio.text:
URL Source: https://www.philschmid.de/cloud-attention/issue-19

Published Time: 2024-07-21

Markdown Content:
Disclaimer: This content is AI generated using different social channels, news and web search.

This week's AI landscape is buzzing with groundbreaking releases from tech giants, innovative research in synthetic data, and practical insights for LLM applications.

## News

### Apple Unveils Open-Source 7B LLM

Apple has entered the AI arena with a bang, releasing a [7B open-source LLM](https://huggingface.co/apple/DCLM-7B) complete with weights, training code, and dataset. Trained on 2.5T tokens from open datasets, this English-focused model boasts a 2048 context window and outperforms Mistral on the MMLU benchmark. The model's open license and impressive performance make it a compelling option for researchers and developers alike.

### Mistral AI Launches 12B Open LLM Powerhouse

Mistral is turning heads with their latest release: a [12B open LLM](https://huggingface.co/mistralai/Mistral-Nemo-Base-2407) that's multilingual and packs a 128k context window. Dubbed Mistral Nemo, this model comes in base and instruct flavors, supporting nine languages and featuring quantized aware-training for FP8 inference. This powerhouse, trained on 3,072 H100 80GB GPUs, sets a new standard for open-source language models.

### Five New AI Models Released in One Day

The AI world saw a flurry of activity with five new model releases in a single day. DeepSeek and Nemo Mistral updated their LLMs, while Deepset, Mixbread, and Snowflake introduced new embedding models. From [DeepSeek's V2-Chat-0628](https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V2-Chat-0628) to [Snowflake's arctic-embed-m-v1.5](https://huggingface.co/Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-m-v1.5), these releases offer improved performance and new capabilities across various domains.

### Hugging Face Introduces Docmatix for Semantic OCR

The Hugging Face science team has unveiled [Docmatix](https://huggingface.co/datasets/HuggingFaceM4/Docmatix), a massive dataset aimed at revolutionizing semantic OCR for PDFs and documents. With 9,500,000 Q/A pairs across 2,444,750 images, this dataset promises to enhance document processing using Vision Language Models (VLMs).

### SmolLM: Bringing AI to Your Pocket

On-device AI takes a leap forward with the [SmolLM series](https://huggingface.co/collections/HuggingFaceTB/smollm-6695016cad7167254ce15966) of small language models. Ranging from 135M to 1.7B parameters, these models are designed to run locally on devices like smartphones. The largest model outperforms Phi-1.5 and Qwen2 1.5B across benchmarks, showcasing the potential for powerful AI in compact packages.

### Mistral's Double Release: Mamba Model and Math Specialist

Markdown Content:
## Microsoft's Copilot Reorganisation

On [17 March 2026](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/17/announcing-copilot-leadership-update/), Satya Nadella announced the most significant restructuring of Microsoft's AI organisation since the company [hired Mustafa Suleyman](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2024/03/19/mustafa-suleyman-deepmind-and-inflection-co-founder-joins-microsoft-to-lead-copilot/) two years earlier. Consumer and commercial Copilot were unified under one leadership team. Suleyman stepped back from day-to-day product leadership to focus on building in-house AI models, and a new generation of executives took the reins. Five days before that, Rajesh Jha, the Executive Vice President (EVP) who had led Microsoft's Experiences + Devices division for years, [announced his retirement](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/12/microsoft-announces-experiences-devices-leadership-changes/) after 35 years at the company.

Illustration: SAMexpert

These are structural changes to how Microsoft builds and delivers AI. They arrived against a backdrop of mixed adoption data, a tech stock downturn, [$37.5 billion in quarterly AI capital expenditure](https://www.theregister.com/2026/02/02/microsoft_ai_spend_copilot/), and a [partnership with OpenAI](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/10/28/the-next-chapter-of-the-microsoft-openai-partnership/) that has evolved from exclusivity towards mutual independence through four publicly renegotiated stages. Here is what happened, who the new leaders are, what their track records tell us about the direction ahead, and what it all means for the organisations that depend on Microsoft's products every day.

## Two separate announcements, five days apart

On [12 March 2026](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2026/03/12/microsoft-announces-experiences-devices-leadership-changes/), Microsoft announced that Rajesh Jha, Executive Vice President of Experiences + Devices, would retire after 35 years at the company. He will step down on 1 July 2026 and stay on in an advisory role. Jha had led the Experiences + Devices division responsible for [Microsoft 365 Copilot](https://samexpert.com/microsoft-365-copilot-licensing/), Windows, and Office. His responsibilities were divided among four EVPs reporting directly to Nadella: Perry Clarke, Charles Lamanna, Pavan Davuluri, and Ryan Roslansky. Jeff Teper was promoted to EVP, and Sumit Chauhan and Kirk Koenigsbauer were promoted to President. Jha's memo said the full cascade of organisational details would be finalised between March and June, ready for the start of FY27 (Microsoft's fiscal year 2027, beginning 1 July 2026).
